title: Bombacopsis nervosa
taxonomy: Bombacopsis nervosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to Central and South America. This plant is classified as a member of the genus Bombacopsis, which is a group of trees that are known for their large, showy flowers and long, straight trunks. The family Malvaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes hibiscus, okra, and cotton. Bombacopsis nervosa is often found in tropical and subtropical regions, where it grows in a variety of habitats.
uses: Bombacopsis nervosa, a tropical tree native to the Americas, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. Its bark and leaves are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
